chunk_summary: This section discusses breastfeeding adaptations for infants with oral-motor or anatomic feeding difficulties, referencing clinical protocols and guidelines for managing feeding challenges in preterm, hypotonic, and low birth weight infants.
learning_objectives: Understand the specific breastfeeding challenges faced by infants with oral-motor or anatomic feeding difficulties.
Learn evidence-based strategies for supporting breastfeeding in late preterm and hypotonic infants.
Familiarize with operational guidelines for optimal feeding of low birth weight infants.
Identify key clinical protocols for managing breastfeeding in special populations.
key_facts: ABM Clinical Protocol #10 provides guidance on breastfeeding late preterm and early term infants.
ABM Clinical Protocol #16 focuses on breastfeeding the hypotonic infant, addressing muscle tone challenges.
The NHM Operational Guidelines (2014) emphasize Kangaroo Mother Care (KMC) and optimal feeding for low birth weight infants.
Infant and Young Child Feeding Guidelines (IAP, 2016) provide evidence-based recommendations for feeding practices.
Oral-motor difficulties in infants may require specialized feeding techniques to ensure adequate nutrition and growth.
reference_quote: ABM Clinical Protocol #10: Breastfeeding the Late Preterm and Early Term Infant provides detailed strategies for supporting breastfeeding in this vulnerable population.
document_key_topics: Feeding strategies for preterm and hypotonic infants
Guidelines for low birth weight infant feeding
